name: Label
visual_summary: The Label component is a basic text block used to display a short piece of text. It can be used in places where a label is necessary, such as form labels or instructional text. It supports a `disabled` state.
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready label component with the following characteristics:

Name: Label
Description: The Label component is a basic text block used to display a short piece of text. It can be used in places where a label is necessary, such as form labels or instructional text. It supports a `disabled` state.
Style: light
Interactions: passive
Tags: component, text, label, input, form

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
Reference implementation (adapt don't copy):
// src/components/Label/Label.tsx
// Tremor Label [v0.0.2]

import React from "react"
import * as LabelPrimitives from "@radix-ui/react-label"

import { cx } from "../../utils/cx"

interface LabelProps
  extends React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof LabelPrimitives.Root> {
  disabled?: boolean
}

const Label = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof LabelPrimitives.Root>,
  LabelProps
>(({ className, disabled, ...props }, forwardedRef) => (
  <LabelPrimitives.Root
    ref={forwardedRef}
